books.title: Our Work: Grades 1-2
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

books.title: A Child's Introduction to the Orchestra
books.author: Wilder & Barer
books.rationale: Creative introduction to each instrument of the orchestra through song.
books.noteText: Unavailable for purchase.

books.title: Playlist for Afternoon Listening and Selected Lessons
books.author: Alveary

books.title: Our Hymns, Our Heritage
books.author: David & Barbara Leeman
books.rationale: This student hymnal contains multi-denominational hymns organized by the church year, with brief histories of the music and lyrics, bios of the writers, related Scripture, and other ideas to meditate upon.

books.title: Like a Bird: The Art of the American Slave Song
books.author: Cynthia Grady
books.rationale: This is a collection of African American spirituals, along with their stories and beautiful illustrations.
books.noteText: 1-2 songs from this book are chosen most years.

books.title: Mussorgsky's Pictures at an Exhibition
books.author: Anna Harwell Celenza
books.rationale: This book tells the sad but touching story behind Mussorgysky's famous composition to honor his friend's paintings.
books.noteText: For Composer Study Term 3, choose either Blind Tom OR Modest Mussorgsky's "Pictures at an Exhibition" to study for the first part of the term. This book is optional and is not required to complete the lessons.

books.title: Song in a Rainstorm: The Story of Musical Prodigy Thomas "Blind Tom" Wiggins
books.author: Glenda Armand
books.rationale: "Had he been born in our time," the author writes, "he might have been called an autistic musical savant." Students will enjoy this picture book biography of an incredible American performer and composer during the last half of the 19th century.
books.noteText: For Composer Study Term 3, choose either Blind Tom OR Modest Mussorgsky's "Pictures at an Exhibition" to study for the first part of the term. Blind Tom is recommended for younger students.
